channel.title: Science - Latest - Google News
channel.copyright: Copyright © 2026 Google. All rights reserved. This XML feed is made available solely for the purpose of rendering Google News results within a personal feed reader for personal, non-commercial use. Any other use of the feed is expressly prohibited. By accessing this feed or using these results in any manner whatsoever, you agree to be bound by the foregoing restrictions.

item.title: Earth’s Spin Is Slowing at a Pace Not Seen in Millions of Years—and You Can Guess Why - Gizmodo
item.description:
1. Earth’s Spin Is Slowing at a Pace Not Seen in Millions of Years—and You Can Guess Why  Gizmodo
  2. Earth’s days are getting longer at an unprecedented rate. Climate change is to blame  Scientific American
  3. Days really are dragging! Length of days on Earth is increasing at an 'unprecedented' rate - and scientists say climate change is to blame  Daily Mail
  4. Polar ice melt is slowing Earth's rotation and lengthening days at an unprecedented rate in the last 3.6 million years  La Brújula Verde
  5. Rising sea levels are lengthening Earth’s days at an unprecedented rate  R&D World

item.title: A wildflower survived a historic megadrought. Scientists now think they know how - CNN
item.description:
1. A wildflower survived a historic megadrought. Scientists now think they know how  CNN
  2. Water vanished in California. Here's how one species saved itself.  SFGATE
  3. How a species evolved fast enough to save itself from extinction  The Washington Post
  4. Can species evolve fast enough to survive as the planet heats up?  New Scientist
  5. Wild plants can rapidly evolve to rescue themselves from climate change  UBC Science

item.title: Gene conversion empowers natural selection in a clonal fish species - Nature
item.description:
1. Gene conversion empowers natural selection in a clonal fish species  Nature
  2. This fish has cloned itself for 100,000 years. Scientists just figured out how.  National Geographic

  4. How a Clonal Fish Prevents Genetic Deterioration  BIOENGINEER.ORG
  5. How an unlikely all-female clonal fish species copied and pasted itself free from extinction  Phys.org

item.title: 'Interstellar messenger' 3I/ATLAS could be nearly as old as the universe itself, James Webb telescope reveals - Live Science
item.description:
1. 'Interstellar messenger' 3I/ATLAS could be nearly as old as the universe itself, James Webb telescope reveals  Live Science
  2. Interstellar comet 3I/ATLAS is 'bursting with methanol,' new study finds  Space
  3. An interstellar comet brought a weird chemical mix into the solar system  Mashable
  4. 'A fingerprint from another solar system'. Latest 3I/ATLAS data proves interstellar comet is unlike anything we've seen before  BBC Sky at Night Magazine
  5. 3I/ATLAS is Fading Away, Leaving Us to Ponder Over Its 22 Mysterious Anomalies  Avi Loeb – Medium

item.title: NASA begins building nuclear-powered Dragonfly drone for 2028 launch to Saturn moon Titan - Space
item.description:
1. NASA begins building nuclear-powered Dragonfly drone for 2028 launch to Saturn moon Titan  Space

  3. NASA Officially Begins Testing the Nuclear-Powered Dragonfly Drone for Titan Mission  The Daily Galaxy
  4. NASA's first helicopter headed to another moon: In 2034, Dragonfly will land on Titan, fly through Saturn's icy atmosphere with eight car-sized rotors, and hunt for signs of prebiotic chemistry that could reveal how life began.  CPG Click Petróleo e Gás
  5. NASA Is Sending a Flying Robot to Another World: Here’s Why Wheels Are No Longer Enough  The Economic Times

item.description:
